question: Can maintenance.py (31179t) be decomposed into smaller modules? Which subsystems are extractable as standalone modules?

tools/maintenance_lanes.py.description: Lane hygiene: staleness, metadata, coordination contracts, evidence fields

tools/maintenance_domains.py.description: Domain INDEX/FRONTIER alignment, frontier decay, anxiety zones, DOMEX coverage

tools/maintenance_inventory.py.description: Swarm capability enumeration, bridge files, command availability, inter-swarm connectivity

tools/maintenance_outcomes.py.description: Per-session check result recording, CHRONIC/ACTIONABLE/SILENT classification, trend analysis

tools/maintenance_drift.py.description: Proxy-K token drift monitoring (dirty-tree handling, stale-floor rebaselining), observer baseline age

result.pattern: Pure function injection — each module receives dependencies as parameters, no circular imports

expect: maintenance.py has 4+ extractable subsystems >=100L each. Largest extraction saves >5k tokens. Post-decomposition size <20k tokens.
actual: 5 modules extracted (1086L total). maintenance.py 2448→1537L, ~31179t→~19955t (36% reduction). All checks PASS. Under 20k target CONFIRMED.
diff: Expected 4+ subsystems — got 5. Expected >5k token savings — got ~11k. Expected <20k final — CONFIRMED (19955t). All 3 predictions directionally correct, magnitude exceeded on extraction count and savings.
f_meta17_next: maintenance.py still 4x ceiling. Next extraction targets: check_mission_constraints (~70L), check_session_log_integrity, check_correction_propagation. orient.py at 16623t is the #2 oversized tool.
